title: Secret Handling Policy
body:
## Secret Handling Policy

### 1. Purpose
This policy defines approved practices for storing and handling secrets such as API keys, database credentials, passwords, tokens, and encryption keys within our organization.

### 2. Approved Storage Methods
- **Environment Variables**: Secrets must be injected into applications via environment variables at runtime. Never commit secrets directly into source code.
- **Secrets Management Services**: Use the designated internal secrets vault (Vault Platform) for long-lived secrets. The vault handles automatic rotation and audit logging.
- **Configuration Files with Restricted Permissions**: When environment variables are not feasible, secrets may be placed in configuration files that are encrypted at rest and accessible only via the deployed service's dedicated OS user.

### 3. Forbidden Practices
- **Hardcoding**: Secrets must never be hardcoded in source code, configuration scripts, or container images.
- **Committing to Version Control**: Secrets in any form (including commented-out lines or test fixtures) must not be pushed to Git repositories.
- **Logging or Printing**: Applications must never log secret values, even at debug levels.

### 4. Rotation Requirements
- All long-lived secrets (e.g., service account credentials) must be rotated at least every 90 days.
- Temporary secrets granted for incident response must expire within 24 hours.
- Rotation procedures are automated through the secrets vault; manual rotation is only allowed in break-glass scenarios documented in the incident log.

### 5. Compliance
All teams must review their current secret handling configuration against this policy and update any non-compliant practices immediately. Any violations detected during automated scans will result in a compliance task being generated.

title: Password Reset Procedure
body:
## Password Reset Procedure

### 1. Scope
This article describes the process for resetting user passwords for corporate systems.

### 2. Self-Service Reset
- Navigate to the identity portal at portal.internal.example.com.
- Follow the on-screen password reset wizard after verifying identity via MFA.

### 3. IT Support Reset
- Only IT Support staff can perform a password reset on behalf of a user if self-service fails.
- The reset must be logged in the ITSM system with ticket number.
- Temporary password must be set to a random string delivered securely via out-of-band channel.

### 4. Policy Compliance
All password resets must adhere to the current Password Policy (ART-1005).
